videos.title: Navio de guerra dos EUA 'COMPLETAMENTE DESTRUÍDO', oficiais da Marinha mortos? Irã revida após at...
videos.description:
A Guarda Revolucionária Islâmica do Irã (IRGC) afirmou ter atacado o porta-aviões USS Abraham Lincoln da Marinha dos EUA com mísseis de cruzeiro. Segundo a mídia iraniana, a IRGC disse que quatro mísseis Qader foram lançados contra o navio de guerra, que transporta caças avançados, incluindo F-35 e F/A-18. Teerã já havia alertado que os movimentos navais dos EUA na região estão sob constante vigilância e podem ser alvejados caso entrem no alcance dos mísseis. A alegação surge em meio a uma situação já volátil, com recentes ataques dos EUA supostamente atingindo infraestruturas importantes dentro do Irã, incluindo uma ponte em Karaj. Donald Trump também afirmou que os objetivos militares americanos no Irã estão próximos da conclusão, ao mesmo tempo em que alertou para novos ataques caso nenhum acordo seja alcançado.

#ussabrahamlincoln #irgc #iranmissiles #iranmissilestrike #irannavy #donaldtrump #iranwar2026

Times Of India (TOI) é o jornal diário em inglês mais vendido do mundo.

Os vídeos do Times of India trazem notícias globais, opiniões e análises precisas. Acompanhamos a ascensão global da Índia, seu crescente engajamento com o mundo, o cenário geopolítico em transformação em meio a conflitos e guerras e a ordem mundial emergente.

NOTÍCIAS INTERNACIONAIS | CONFLITOS GLOBAIS | GUERRA NO ORIENTE MÉDIO | MUDANÇAS NA ORDEM MUNDIAL #TOILive | #TOIVideos

Inscreva-se no canal do Times of India no YouTube e clique no ícone do sino para receber notificações quando entrarmos ao vivo.

Junte-se a milhões de leitores do TOI; Baixe nosso aplicativo em http://toi.in/app-yt

Visite nosso site: https://www.timesofindia.com/
Siga-nos no X/ @timesofindia
Facebook:   / timesofindia  
Instagram:   / timesofindia  
Siga o canal do TOI no WhatsApp: https://bit.ly/3RYl0J9
Para notícias e atualizações diárias e reportagens exclusivas, siga o Times of India.
videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Iran has claimed its missiles targeted US Navy's aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln. According to Iranian media, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps IRGC said it targeted the American warship using four Kadair cruise missiles. According to an Associated Press report, the USS Abraham Lincoln hosts multiple squadrons of aircraft, including F-35 Lightning 2 fighter jets and FA18 Super Hornet fighter jets. Meanwhile, the destroyers bring with them hundreds of missiles, which could include dozens of Tomahawk land attack cruise missiles. On March 25th, Iran's army had claimed that it targeted the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier with coastal cruise missiles. According to IRNA, the commander of the Iranian Navy, Rear Admiral Shaham Irani, had said that the movements of the US warship were under continuous monitoring and would be targeted once it entered the range of Iran's missile systems. Earlier, another US warship, the USS Gerald R. Ford, which had been deployed in the Middle East, caught fire last month. The mishap injured sailors and destroyed 100 beds. According to the Guardian, an official said nearly 200 sailors were treated for smoke related injuries when the fire broke out in the warship's main laundry area. Donald Trump had ordered the USS Gerald R. forward into the Middle East from the Caribbean last month to join other warships and military assets the US had built up in the region ahead of the start of the conflict in the Middle East. Earlier, US strikes destroyed the B1 bridge, which was still under construction, killing at least eight people and wounded 95. In a truth social post, Trump shared the video footage of the strike and wrote, "The biggest bridge in Iran comes tumbling down, never to be used again, much more to follow. It is time for Iran to make a deal before it is too late, and there is nothing left of what still could become a great country." Donald Trump has already said that the US military core strategic objectives in Iran were nearing completion. We totally obliterated those nuclear sites. The regime then sought to rebuild their nuclear program at a totally different location, making clear they had no intention of abandoning their pursuit of nuclear weapons. They were also rapidly building a vast stockpile of conventional ballistic missiles and would soon have had missiles that could reach the American homeland, Europe, and virtually any other place on Earth. Iran's strategy was so obvious. They wanted to produce as many missiles as possible and they did with the longest range possible and they had some weapons that nobody believed they had. We just learned that out. We took them out. We took them all out. So that no one would really dare stop them. And their race for a nuclear bomb, a nuclear weapon, a nuclear weapon like nobody's ever seen before. They were right at the doorstep. For years, everyone has said that Iran cannot have nuclear weapons. But in the end, those are just words if you're not willing to take action when the time comes. As I stated in my announcement of Operation Epic Fury, our objectives are very simple and clear. We are systematically dismantling the regime's ability to threaten America or project power outside of their borders. That means eliminating Iran's navy, which is now absolutely destroyed, hurting their air force and their missile program at levels never seen before, and annihilating their defense industrial base. We've done all of it. Their navy is gone. Their air force is gone. Their missiles are just about used up or beaten. Taken together, these actions will Iran military, crush their ability to support terrorist proxies, and deny them the ability to build a nuclear bomb. Our armed forces have been extraordinary. There's never been anything like it militarily. Everyone is talking about it. And tonight, I'm pleased to say that these core strategic objectives are nearing completion. As we celebrate this progress, we think especially of the 13 American warriors who have laid down their lives in this fight to prevent our children from ever having to face a nuclear Iran. Thanks to the progress we've made, I can say tonight that we are on track to complete all of America's military objectives shortly. Very shortly, we are going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. In the meantime, discussions are ongoing. Regime change was not our goal. We never said regime change, but regime change has occurred because of all of their original leaders death. They're all dead. The new group is less radical and much more reasonable. Yet, if during this period of time, no deal is made. We have our eyes on key targets. If there is no deal, we are going to hit each and every one of their electric generating plants very hard and probably simultaneously. We have not hit their oil, even though that's the easiest target of all because it would not give them even a small chance of survival or rebuilding. But we could hit it and it would be gone and there's not a thing they could do about it. They have no anti-aircraft equipment. Their radar is 100% annihilated. We are unstoppable as a military force. The nuclear sites that we obliterated with the B2 bombers have been hit so hard that it would take months to get near the nuclear dust and we have it under intense satellite surveillance and control. If we see them make a move, even a move for it will hit them with missiles very hard. Again, we have all the cards. They have none. It's very important that we keep this conflict in perspective. News never stops. The world keeps turning and every turn tells a story. Wars, elections, geopolitics, Davids versus Goliaths. While everyone is celebrating the US China trade thaw, Trump has been quietly doing something else across Asia. Were once home to the ancient silk road connecting east and west. But who's connecting the dots? Who's decoding the global storm? Welcome to Times of India videos. You're watching a special edition of Global Pulse with me, Niha Kana. We decode the shifting grains of geopolitics to you. From Washington to Wuhan, everything all at once. Just two years ago, Canada was one of the most popular countries for Indian students. From Davos to Delhi, we track the decisions that move markets, shift policies, and shape the future. No jargon, no bias, just clear, credible global journalism. Because understanding the world doesn't have to be complicated. It just has to be smart. And that's what we do best. At Times of India videos, we don't just tell stories. We build perspective. We connect the dots between what's global and what's personal. What's happening out there and what it means for you. It's fast. It's fearless. It's making sense of the changing world order. Times of India videos because your world view is just a tap away.

videos.title: 'PILOTO MORTO, AVIÃO CAIU': Irã explode 'outro' caça F-35 dos EUA? 'PROVAS' CHOCANTES SÃO DIVULGADAS
videos.description:
A tensão aumenta no Oriente Médio após o Irã afirmar que um segundo caça furtivo F-35 dos EUA foi abatido sobre a região central do país. A mídia estatal iraniana, incluindo a Press TV e a agência de notícias Mehr, divulgou fotos do suposto local da queda, embora a autenticidade ainda não tenha sido verificada. A Guarda Revolucionária Islâmica (IRGC) afirma que a aeronave foi atingida por seu sistema de defesa aérea e que é improvável que o piloto tenha sobrevivido. O Comando Central dos EUA não confirmou o incidente. Se confirmado, a perda de um F-35 de última geração representaria um golpe significativo para as operações aéreas dos EUA na região. O acompanhamento da situação continua.

#Irã #F35 #ForçasMilitarEUA #IRGC #TensõesNoOrienteMédio #CaçaFurtivo #DefesaAérea #ÚltimasNotícias

The IRGC has made similar claims in the past, including earlier reports of an F-35 being damaged by Iranian air defenses. Independent verification from outside the region is still pending. The F-35 Lightning 2 is one of the most advanced fifth generation fighter jets in the world. central to US and allied air operations. Losses of such aircraft, if confirmed, would represent a significant moment in the monthsl long conflict and a challenge to US assertions of air dominance. According to the IRGC, the operation was carried out jointly by its aerospace force and navy targeting what it described as US- linked industrial and military infrastructure across the Gulf. The statement said the strikes hit US associated steel and aluminum facilities in Abu Dhabi. Additional industrial and logistical sites in Bahrain, remaining operational sections of previously targeted aluminum infrastructure, facilities linked to Israeli defense firm Rafael Advanced Defense Systems, and a location described as a US forces hideout near Manama. The IRGC framed the attack as both retaliation and warning. Thanks to the progress we've made, I can say tonight that we are on track to complete all of America's military objectives shortly. Trump's warning that Iran could be bombed back to the stone age. Thran did not hold back. In a fiery message, the Iranian embassy in Japan hit back hard. It accused Trump of promoting savagery and wararmongering. The statement was blunt and personal. It said in today's civilized world such rhetoric has no place and then came the direct jab. It told Trump that stone age behavior is exactly where he belongs. This is a dramatic escalation in tone and it signals how tense the situation has become. Iran's response came as Trump reiterated his threat to blow Iran back to the stone age. Thanks to the progress we've made, I can say tonight that we are on track to complete all of America's military objectives shortly. Very shortly, we are going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. In the meantime, discussions are ongoing. Regime change was not our goal. We never said regime change, but regime change has occurred because of all of their original leaders death. They're all dead. The new group is less radical and much more reasonable. Yet, if during this period of time no deal is made, we have our eyes on key targets. If there is no deal, we are going to hit each and every one of their electric generating plants very hard and probably simultaneously. We have not hit their oil, even though that's the easiest target of all because it would not give them even a small chance of survival or rebuilding. But we could hit it and it would be gone and there's not a thing they could do about it. They have no anti-aircraft equipment. Their radar is 100% annihilated. We are unstoppable as a military force. The nuclear sites that we obliterated with the B2 bombers have been hit so hard that it would take months to get near the nuclear dust. And we have it under intense satellite surveillance and control. If we see them make a move, even a move for it will hit them with missiles very hard. Again, we have all the cards. They have none. It's very important that we keep this conflict in perspective. Back in the United States, the backlash is already building. Congresswoman Yasan Ansari has strongly condemned Trump's remarks. She called the statement vile, horrifying, and deeply disturbing. She also reminded people of the scale of what was being said. Trump was talking about a country of 90 million people. That line is now fueling outrage across political circles. But the impact is not just political. It is already hitting global markets. Investors reacted within minutes of Trump's speech. US stock futures dropped sharply. European markets followed with even steeper losses. In Asia, the shock waves were clear. Japan's Nicay index fell nearly 2%. South Korea's Cosby plunged even further. At the same time, oil prices surged and the US dollar strengthened as uncertainty grew. This is how global fear plays out. Markets fall, oil rises, and tensions climb even higher. Now the big question remains. Is this just rhetoric or is it a signal of what comes next? Because when words like stone age enter the conversation, the stakes are no longer just strategic. They become existential and the world is watching closely, waiting for the next move.

And we have it under intense satellite surveillance and control. If we see them make a move, even a move for it, we'll hit them with missiles very hard. Again, we have all the cards. They have none. It's very important that we keep this conflict in perspective. American involvement in World War I lasted one year, 7 months, and five days. World War II lasted for three years, eight months, and 25 days. The Korean War lasted for three years, one month, and two days. The Vietnam War lasted for 19 years, 5 months, and 29 days. videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição President Putin and his press secretary Dmitri Pascov have already commented on the topic that is now being blown up in the media about Russia allegedly supplying intelligence data to Iran. And they stated that we indeed have very close relations with Iran, including an agreement on military technical cooperation. We have supplied specific military products to Iran. But Russia's third reported help to Iran in the war against Israel, and the USA raises fresh questions about how far Moscow is willing to go to back tan's regional ambitions and weaken Western influence. The Russian army's drone tactics appear to be inspiring Iranian proxies against Israel, showing how battlefield methods from one war are being quickly copied and adapted in another. After North Korean missiles, Hezbollah has now unleashed Russian style hell on the IDF, combining imported tactics and upgraded weapons to intensify pressure on Israeli forces. Hezbollah operatives have been increasingly using firstperson view or FPV drones in warfare, adding a fast, precise, and harder to stop tool to their expanding battlefield arsenal. The targets have been Israeli troops carrying out a ground offensive in southern Lebanon, where these drones are being used to hit forces moving through a highly contested and dangerous front line. Recent clips published by Hezbollah show small FPV drones carrying an explosive charge, highlighting how compact systems can now be turned into lethal strike weapons with surprising effectiveness. The clips also show the drones striking Israeli tanks and other vehicles in southern Lebanon, underscoring the growing threat these weapons pose to armored units and mobile ground operations. Notably, some of the drones are guided using a spool of fiber optic cable, a method that gives operators a direct connection to the aircraft and makes it far harder for enemies to break the link in real time. This modification protects the signal from being electronically jammed, allowing the drones to keep flying and striking even in environments where conventional radiocrolled systems might be knocked out. The IDF reported several injuries among troops as a result of Hezbollah drone attacks, underscoring how these small but precise weapons are already inflicting real battlefield damage on Israeli forces. At least 10 Israeli soldiers have been killed in fighting against Hezbollah since March 2nd, a grim sign that the conflict on the northern front has become steadily more lethal and harder to contain. Crucially, FPV drones are known as a hallmark of the war between Russia and Ukraine, where their speed, precision, and low cost have transformed them into one of the most feared tools of modern combat. Earlier, the US and Europe claimed Russia was also helping Iran with military intelligence, adding to suspicions that Moscow may be quietly backing Thran's war effort. We have supplied specific military products to Iran. But we cannot agree with accusations that we are helping Iran by transferring intelligence data. You said it's intelligence data on the locations of American bases. The coordinates of American military bases in the region are known to everyone. This is not hidden. It is open information and that Iran attacks these military bases does not personally surprise me. We never initiate any actions to take something away from someone. And we never rejoice when wars initiated by others by other states lead to changes in the world markets resulting in rising prices for energy carriers and other goods that the Russian Federation exports. We will always regardless of anything trade engage in economic relations with those interested in it. We see the interest of the United States as they have stated as they have official documents or speeches by officials stating that they will strive for dominance in the global energy markets. Venezuela is a direct example. They talked about the need to remove the regime of drug dealers. Ultimately, it ended with the United States taking control of Venezuela's oil industry. The same is happening with Iran. President Trump essentially said directly that he wants to control the strait of Hormuz together with Iran. All hydrocarbon supplies passing through this straight. The Nordstreams that Ukrainian saboturs blew up with apparent support from Western intelligence services. No one condemned this act, neither France nor Germany, which I consider simply disgraceful for a country like Germany to swallow this subversive terrorist act against their core interests. And now the United States is saying that they want to take the Nordstreams for themselves. And if you look, sanctions were imposed against Russian companies Lucy and Ros. Their entire overseas business is the target of hostile takeover attempts. So we are not using our actions on the international stage to try to extract illegal gains. We will always maintain relationships with partners who adhere to the same principle unlike the European Union which decided that it needs to terminate all contracts and other agreements with the Russian Federation out of sheer desire as desired by both Paris and Berlin and of course your friends in the Baltic States and in Poland which I cannot fail to mention. We cannot understand or support the position of those countries forced to comply with these absolutely unlawful restrictions. Russia's Chchen military units have reportedly announced their readiness to deploy to Iran, a development that would further expand the conflict's international dimension and deepen Western concerns. In October 2025, Russia used a firstperson view drone to hit eastern Ukraine for the first time since the war began, marking a significant shift in battlefield tactics and showing how quickly drone warfare continues to evolve. Unlike radiocontrol drones, FPV drones operate via long spools of fiber optic wire, which gives operators a direct link to the aircraft and makes the system harder to disrupt in contested airspace. FPV drones are immune to jamming and to terrain features that can block line of sight radio signals, making them especially dangerous in modern wars where electronic warfare is often used to knock drones off course. The drones pose a huge threat because of their resilience, and their range of operation is only increasing, which means they can reach deeper into enemy positions and stay effective even in heavily defended environments. The increasing range of FPV drones extends the depth of front lines, putting civilians at risk as areas once considered relatively safe now fall within striking distance of these small but highly adaptable weapons. Hezbollah also carried out several attacks with FPV drones during the war in 2024, showing that this technology is no longer limited to state militaries and is now being embraced by armed groups across different conflict zones. for the good of our nation. videos.title: Trump Gets a Major Shock as Iranian Media Claims US F-15E Was Shot Down and Pilot Captured?
videos.description:
Unverified reports claim a US F-15E fighter jet has been shot down over Iran, with speculation that the pilot may have been captured. Iranian-linked media and IRGC sources suggest the aircraft was hit during escalating strikes under “Operation True Promise 4,” though Washington has not confirmed the claims. Meanwhile, missile attacks have hit key targets across the region, including bases and critical infrastructure. Despite intensifying conflict, shipping through the Strait of Hormuz continues. If confirmed, the downing of a US warplane could mark a major escalation in the already volatile Middle East crisis.

videos.title: "Netanyahu enganou o Trump" | Robinson Farinazzo no Juca Kfouri Entrevista
videos.description:
Juca Kfouri recebe Robinson Farinazzo, capitão de fragata da reserva da Marinha e especialista em inteligência, para uma análise profunda dos conflitos que estão redesenhando o mapa do poder global.

Neste vídeo, Farinazzo discute a possibilidade de uma Terceira Guerra Mundial, a resiliência tecnológica do Irã diante das pressões de Trump e Netanyahu, e o impacto direto dessas crises na economia brasileira, do preço dos combustíveis à questão dos fertilizantes. O capitão também faz um alerta contundente sobre a sabotagem histórica do programa nuclear brasileiro e a importância da defesa para a manutenção da soberania nacional. Uma aula sobre estratégia, tecnologia militar e os bastidores do poder no Sul Global.

#Geopolitica #DefesaNacional #JucaKfouri #RobsonFarinazzo #Irã #Estados Unidos #SoberaniaBrasil #GuerraMundial #NoticiasInternacionais #TecnologiaMilitar #OrienteMedio #EconomiaGlobal #PoliticaExterna #InteligenciaMilitar #Estrategia

videos.title: #aovivo Irã abate 2º caça F-35 dos EUA - Trump ameaça "destruir o que resta" se não houver acordo
videos.description:
Ao vivo agora no TVT News. Moraes e Toffoli viajaram em jatinho de Vorcaro, Irã abate F-35 americano e as principais notícias do Brasil e do mundo.

Na Sexta-feira Santa, Trump voltou a escalar as ameaças contra o Irã, afirmando que o exército americano ainda não começou a destruir o que resta da infraestrutura iraniana. O Irã anunciou ter abatido o segundo caça F-35 dos EUA, aeronave que pode custar até meio bilhão de reais a unidade. Um ataque iraniano também atingiu a operação de nuvem da Amazon no Bahrein, danificando a infraestrutura da gigante de tecnologia. As baixas americanas na guerra chegam a 750, entre mortos e feridos, segundo o The Intercept.

Trump demitiu a secretária de Justiça Pam Bondi após 14 meses de mandato marcados por polêmicas, incluindo a condução do caso Epstein. O secretário de Defesa Pete Hegseth demitiu o chefe do Estado-Maior do Exército e outros dois generais, totalizando três baixas militares de alta patente em um único dia. No Líbano, ataques de Israel colapsaram o sistema de saúde, com mais de 50 trabalhadores da saúde mortos e cinco hospitais fora de funcionamento.

Revelação bombástica: os ministros Alexandre de Moraes e Dias Toffoli utilizaram aeronaves do banqueiro Daniel Vorcaro em deslocamentos repetidos, inclusive com familiares. A informação aumenta a resistência de ministros à proposta de código de conduta do STF. O presidente da corte Fachin defendeu regras de transparência até o fim do ano, mas bastidores descrevem o tribunal como uma nau à deriva.

Flávio Bolsonaro teve que recorrer ao ChatGPT para saber seu próprio posicionamento sobre o PIX, após ser acusado de fazer campanha contra o sistema nos EUA. Lula defendeu o PIX e acusou Flávio de buscar interferência externa. Michelle Bolsonaro repostou vídeo de Esperidião Amin, adversário de Carlos Bolsonaro no Senado de Santa Catarina, aumentando o mal-estar na família.

📌 Destaques do dia

videos.title: A surpreendente estratégia de guerra do Irã - Robinson Farinazzo - Programa 20 Minutos
videos.description:
A surpreendente estratégia de guerra do Irã - Robinson Farinazzo - Programa 20 Minutos

🌍 Neste episódio urgente do Programa 20 Minutos, dissecamos a mais nova e ousada jogada do tabuleiro geopolítico global: o fechamento do Estreito de Hormuz pelo Irã como resposta direta à guerra declarada por Donald Trump contra a nação persa em fevereiro de 2026. Enquanto o mundo assistia a uma possível escalada convencional, o Irã surpreendeu ao acionar sua arma mais letal — o gargalo por onde escoa cerca de 20% do petróleo mundial — transformando um ponto estratégico em instrumento de asfixia econômica global. Com a análise contundente de Robinson Farinazzo, vamos entender como essa estratégia de guerra assimétrica não apenas neutralizou a superioridade militar americana na região, mas também expôs a fragilidade da cadeia de suprimentos ocidental, elevando o preço do barril e forçando potências mundiais a repensarem suas alianças. Descubra os bastidores dessa decisão, os riscos de uma conflagração maior e como o Irã, ao fechar a torneira do Golfo Pérsico, redefiniu as regras do conflito e desafiou a hegemonia dos Estados Unidos no Oriente Médio de forma avassaladora. Esta é uma aula de geopolítica que você não pode perder.

🚀 ASSISTA AGORA e entenda como o Irã travou o mundo com uma única manobra!

videos.title: "Netanyahu enganou o Trump" | Robinson Farinazzo no Juca Kfouri Entrevista
videos.description:
Juca Kfouri recebe Robinson Farinazzo, capitão de fragata da reserva da Marinha e especialista em inteligência, para uma análise profunda dos conflitos que estão redesenhando o mapa do poder global.

Neste vídeo, Farinazzo discute a possibilidade de uma Terceira Guerra Mundial, a resiliência tecnológica do Irã diante das pressões de Trump e Netanyahu, e o impacto direto dessas crises na economia brasileira, do preço dos combustíveis à questão dos fertilizantes. O capitão também faz um alerta contundente sobre a sabotagem histórica do programa nuclear brasileiro e a importância da defesa para a manutenção da soberania nacional. Uma aula sobre estratégia, tecnologia militar e os bastidores do poder no Sul Global.

videos.title: #aovivo Irã abate 2º caça F-35 dos EUA - Trump ameaça "destruir o que resta" se não houver acordo
videos.description:
Ao vivo agora no TVT News. Moraes e Toffoli viajaram em jatinho de Vorcaro, Irã abate F-35 americano e as principais notícias do Brasil e do mundo.

Na Sexta-feira Santa, Trump voltou a escalar as ameaças contra o Irã, afirmando que o exército americano ainda não começou a destruir o que resta da infraestrutura iraniana. O Irã anunciou ter abatido o segundo caça F-35 dos EUA, aeronave que pode custar até meio bilhão de reais a unidade. Um ataque iraniano também atingiu a operação de nuvem da Amazon no Bahrein, danificando a infraestrutura da gigante de tecnologia. As baixas americanas na guerra chegam a 750, entre mortos e feridos, segundo o The Intercept.

Trump demitiu a secretária de Justiça Pam Bondi após 14 meses de mandato marcados por polêmicas, incluindo a condução do caso Epstein. O secretário de Defesa Pete Hegseth demitiu o chefe do Estado-Maior do Exército e outros dois generais, totalizando três baixas militares de alta patente em um único dia. No Líbano, ataques de Israel colapsaram o sistema de saúde, com mais de 50 trabalhadores da saúde mortos e cinco hospitais fora de funcionamento.

Revelação bombástica: os ministros Alexandre de Moraes e Dias Toffoli utilizaram aeronaves do banqueiro Daniel Vorcaro em deslocamentos repetidos, inclusive com familiares. A informação aumenta a resistência de ministros à proposta de código de conduta do STF. O presidente da corte Fachin defendeu regras de transparência até o fim do ano, mas bastidores descrevem o tribunal como uma nau à deriva.

Flávio Bolsonaro teve que recorrer ao ChatGPT para saber seu próprio posicionamento sobre o PIX, após ser acusado de fazer campanha contra o sistema nos EUA. Lula defendeu o PIX e acusou Flávio de buscar interferência externa. Michelle Bolsonaro repostou vídeo de Esperidião Amin, adversário de Carlos Bolsonaro no Senado de Santa Catarina, aumentando o mal-estar na família.

videos.title: A surpreendente estratégia de guerra do Irã - Robinson Farinazzo - Programa 20 Minutos
videos.description:
A surpreendente estratégia de guerra do Irã - Robinson Farinazzo - Programa 20 Minutos

🌍 Neste episódio urgente do Programa 20 Minutos, dissecamos a mais nova e ousada jogada do tabuleiro geopolítico global: o fechamento do Estreito de Hormuz pelo Irã como resposta direta à guerra declarada por Donald Trump contra a nação persa em fevereiro de 2026. Enquanto o mundo assistia a uma possível escalada convencional, o Irã surpreendeu ao acionar sua arma mais letal — o gargalo por onde escoa cerca de 20% do petróleo mundial — transformando um ponto estratégico em instrumento de asfixia econômica global. Com a análise contundente de Robinson Farinazzo, vamos entender como essa estratégia de guerra assimétrica não apenas neutralizou a superioridade militar americana na região, mas também expôs a fragilidade da cadeia de suprimentos ocidental, elevando o preço do barril e forçando potências mundiais a repensarem suas alianças. Descubra os bastidores dessa decisão, os riscos de uma conflagração maior e como o Irã, ao fechar a torneira do Golfo Pérsico, redefiniu as regras do conflito e desafiou a hegemonia dos Estados Unidos no Oriente Médio de forma avassaladora. Esta é uma aula de geopolítica que você não pode perder.

videos.title: "Netanyahu enganou o Trump" | Robinson Farinazzo no Juca Kfouri Entrevista
videos.description:
Juca Kfouri recebe Robinson Farinazzo, capitão de fragata da reserva da Marinha e especialista em inteligência, para uma análise profunda dos conflitos que estão redesenhando o mapa do poder global.

Neste vídeo, Farinazzo discute a possibilidade de uma Terceira Guerra Mundial, a resiliência tecnológica do Irã diante das pressões de Trump e Netanyahu, e o impacto direto dessas crises na economia brasileira, do preço dos combustíveis à questão dos fertilizantes. O capitão também faz um alerta contundente sobre a sabotagem histórica do programa nuclear brasileiro e a importância da defesa para a manutenção da soberania nacional. Uma aula sobre estratégia, tecnologia militar e os bastidores do poder no Sul Global.

videos.title: #aovivo Irã abate 2º caça F-35 dos EUA - Trump ameaça "destruir o que resta" se não houver acordo
videos.description:
Ao vivo agora no TVT News. Moraes e Toffoli viajaram em jatinho de Vorcaro, Irã abate F-35 americano e as principais notícias do Brasil e do mundo.

Na Sexta-feira Santa, Trump voltou a escalar as ameaças contra o Irã, afirmando que o exército americano ainda não começou a destruir o que resta da infraestrutura iraniana. O Irã anunciou ter abatido o segundo caça F-35 dos EUA, aeronave que pode custar até meio bilhão de reais a unidade. Um ataque iraniano também atingiu a operação de nuvem da Amazon no Bahrein, danificando a infraestrutura da gigante de tecnologia. As baixas americanas na guerra chegam a 750, entre mortos e feridos, segundo o The Intercept.

Trump demitiu a secretária de Justiça Pam Bondi após 14 meses de mandato marcados por polêmicas, incluindo a condução do caso Epstein. O secretário de Defesa Pete Hegseth demitiu o chefe do Estado-Maior do Exército e outros dois generais, totalizando três baixas militares de alta patente em um único dia. No Líbano, ataques de Israel colapsaram o sistema de saúde, com mais de 50 trabalhadores da saúde mortos e cinco hospitais fora de funcionamento.

Revelação bombástica: os ministros Alexandre de Moraes e Dias Toffoli utilizaram aeronaves do banqueiro Daniel Vorcaro em deslocamentos repetidos, inclusive com familiares. A informação aumenta a resistência de ministros à proposta de código de conduta do STF. O presidente da corte Fachin defendeu regras de transparência até o fim do ano, mas bastidores descrevem o tribunal como uma nau à deriva.

Flávio Bolsonaro teve que recorrer ao ChatGPT para saber seu próprio posicionamento sobre o PIX, após ser acusado de fazer campanha contra o sistema nos EUA. Lula defendeu o PIX e acusou Flávio de buscar interferência externa. Michelle Bolsonaro repostou vídeo de Esperidião Amin, adversário de Carlos Bolsonaro no Senado de Santa Catarina, aumentando o mal-estar na família.

📌 Destaques do dia
✈️ Moraes e Toffoli viajaram em jatinho de Vorcaro com familiares
🛩️ Irã abate segundo F-35 americano durante a guerra
💻 Ataque iraniano destrói infraestrutura da Amazon no Bahrein
💥 Trump ameaça destruir o que resta do Irã
👮 Trump demite secretária de Justiça e três generais
🏥 Israel colapsa sistema de saúde no Líbano com 50 profissionais mortos
📱 Flávio recorre ao ChatGPT para saber seu posicionamento sobre o PIX

videos.title: POSSE DE BOLA AO VIVO COM MAURO CEZAR, ARNALDO RIBEIRO, TIRONI, JUCA KFOURI, LAVIERI E TRAJANO
videos.description:
Mixto x Internacional : Canal UOL transmite às 16h duelo do Brasileirão Feminino. Acompanhe ao vivo pelo YouTube aqui:    • AO VIVO: MIXTO X INTERNACIONAL | BRASILEIR...  

O Posse de Bola desta sexta-feira (03) analisa a vitória do Palmeiras e a excelente atuação de Marlon Freitas, que amplia a distância competitiva em relação ao Flamengo. O programa debate a pressão extrema sobre Dorival Jr no Corinthians, a instabilidade de Roger no São Paulo e as críticas ao desempenho de Lucas Paquetá no Flamengo. Além disso, destacamos o retorno de Neymar ao Santos, participando dos gols na vitória contra o Remo, mas gerando polêmica com um cartão amarelo.

Arnaldo Ribeiro, Eduardo Tironi, Mauro Cezar, José Trajano, Juca Kfouri e Danilo Lavieri debatem os principais assuntos do futebol brasileiro. Ao vivo toda segunda e sexta-feira às 8h30.

videos.title: POSSE DE BOLA AO VIVO COM MAURO CEZAR, ARNALDO RIBEIRO, TIRONI, JUCA KFOURI, LAVIERI E TRAJANO
videos.description:
Mixto x Internacional : Canal UOL transmite às 16h duelo do Brasileirão Feminino. Acompanhe ao vivo pelo YouTube aqui:    • AO VIVO: MIXTO X INTERNACIONAL | BRASILEIR...  

O Posse de Bola desta sexta-feira (03) analisa a vitória do Palmeiras e a excelente atuação de Marlon Freitas, que amplia a distância competitiva em relação ao Flamengo. O programa debate a pressão extrema sobre Dorival Jr no Corinthians, a instabilidade de Roger no São Paulo e as críticas ao desempenho de Lucas Paquetá no Flamengo. Além disso, destacamos o retorno de Neymar ao Santos, participando dos gols na vitória contra o Remo, mas gerando polêmica com um cartão amarelo.

Arnaldo Ribeiro, Eduardo Tironi, Mauro Cezar, José Trajano, Juca Kfouri e Danilo Lavieri debatem os principais assuntos do futebol brasileiro. Ao vivo toda segunda e sexta-feira às 8h30.

#aovivo #canaluol #possedebola #futebol #SeleçãoBrasileira #Palmeiras #Corinthians #SantosFC #Neymar #DorivalJunior #Flamengo #UOLPosseDeBola #FutebolBrasileiro #Brasileirão2026 #Paquetá #SaoPauloFC

videos.title: POSSE DE BOLA AO VIVO COM MAURO CEZAR, ARNALDO RIBEIRO, TIRONI, JUCA KFOURI, LAVIERI E TRAJANO
videos.description:
Mixto x Internacional : Canal UOL transmite às 16h duelo do Brasileirão Feminino. Acompanhe ao vivo pelo YouTube aqui:    • AO VIVO: MIXTO X INTERNACIONAL | BRASILEIR...  

O Posse de Bola desta sexta-feira (03) analisa a vitória do Palmeiras e a excelente atuação de Marlon Freitas, que amplia a distância competitiva em relação ao Flamengo. O programa debate a pressão extrema sobre Dorival Jr no Corinthians, a instabilidade de Roger no São Paulo e as críticas ao desempenho de Lucas Paquetá no Flamengo. Além disso, destacamos o retorno de Neymar ao Santos, participando dos gols na vitória contra o Remo, mas gerando polêmica com um cartão amarelo.

Arnaldo Ribeiro, Eduardo Tironi, Mauro Cezar, José Trajano, Juca Kfouri e Danilo Lavieri debatem os principais assuntos do futebol brasileiro. Ao vivo toda segunda e sexta-feira às 8h30.

videos.title: POSSE DE BOLA AO VIVO COM MAURO CEZAR, ARNALDO RIBEIRO, TIRONI, JUCA KFOURI, LAVIERI E TRAJANO
videos.description:
Mixto x Internacional : Canal UOL transmite às 16h duelo do Brasileirão Feminino. Acompanhe ao vivo pelo YouTube aqui:    • AO VIVO: MIXTO X INTERNACIONAL | BRASILEIR...  

O Posse de Bola desta sexta-feira (03) analisa a vitória do Palmeiras e a excelente atuação de Marlon Freitas, que amplia a distância competitiva em relação ao Flamengo. O programa debate a pressão extrema sobre Dorival Jr no Corinthians, a instabilidade de Roger no São Paulo e as críticas ao desempenho de Lucas Paquetá no Flamengo. Além disso, destacamos o retorno de Neymar ao Santos, participando dos gols na vitória contra o Remo, mas gerando polêmica com um cartão amarelo.

Arnaldo Ribeiro, Eduardo Tironi, Mauro Cezar, José Trajano, Juca Kfouri e Danilo Lavieri debatem os principais assuntos do futebol brasileiro. Ao vivo toda segunda e sexta-feira às 8h30.

#aovivo #canaluol #possedebola #futebol #SeleçãoBrasileira #Palmeiras #Corinthians #SantosFC #Neymar #DorivalJunior #Flamengo #UOLPosseDeBola #FutebolBrasileiro #Brasileirão2026 #Paquetá #SaoPauloFC
